name: Granny's Broth (Cawl Mamgu)
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Best end of neck Welsh lamb
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the meat in a saucepan and cover with cold water. Add salt and pepper, then bring to a simmer over low heat.
2. Cook slowly for 2-2.5 hours, adding the carrots (halved), swede (sliced), and leeks (white parts) during this time.
3. Add potatoes (quartered) and continue cooking for another 30 minutes.
4. Thicken the broth with flour and a little water when the potatoes are nearly done.
5. Add the chopped parsley and remaining leek green parts, then simmer for an additional 10 minutes before serving in basins while hot.
Cawl was traditionally served as dinner on farms during winter months in South and West Wales. The broth is served in basins or bowls with bread, and meat and vegetables are served as a second course.
